what_it_does: Defines a browser IIFE that mirrors CV_REGISTRY types into legacy global arrays for widget kinds, widget systems, wizard kinds, and step kinds, annotates WS_BLOCKS entries with type IDs, dispatches rebuild events, schedules rebuilds during boot and registry changes, and exposes global save-as-type and prompt helpers.
observations: Exits early with a console warning when window.CV_REGISTRY is absent.
Converts registry Type objects into the object shapes expected by legacy widget, system, wizard, and step consumers.
Each rebuild snapshots original legacy arrays, emits registry-derived entries first, then appends unseen legacy entries tagged with provenance "built-in".
Wizard kind shape is inferred from tags with a diamond fallback, and missing steps can be hydrated from the saved legacy wizard kind.
WS_BLOCKS is not replaced; each existing block definition is annotated with a _typeId derived from its key.
Rebuild notifications are sent through a cv-registry-rebuild CustomEvent.
Boot logic polls briefly for legacy globals while registry changes are handled through R.subscribe and requestAnimationFrame scheduling.
CV_TYPES_SAVE delegates promotion work to window.CV_TYPES_VI.promoteInstance for widgets, wizards, wizard steps, and blocks.
CV_TYPES_PROMPT keeps an in-memory Set of listeners and catches listener errors silently.
standouts: Registry-to-legacy mirroring is implemented without requiring legacy consumers to change their read paths.
The adapter preserves existing legacy entries while allowing registry entries with matching IDs to supply current metadata.
Boot timing is handled with a short retry loop plus event-style scheduling for later registry changes.
conventions: Uses an IIFE and window globals rather than module imports or exports.
Keeps conversion helpers small and shape-specific.
Uses optional chaining for nested Type fields and simple fallbacks for IDs, labels, descriptions, dimensions, and provenance.
Uses Set instances for de-duplication and listener registration.
Mutates global arrays and WS_BLOCKS definitions in place through window-scoped names.
concerns: CV_TYPES_SAVE assumes window.CV_TYPES_VI and promoteInstance exist; this file does not guard those calls.
idTail assumes the provided id is a string containing split-compatible data.
CV_TYPES_PROMPT.open catches and ignores subscriber errors, which can hide listener failures from callers.
The boot interval can call rebuild repeatedly until both widget and wizard globals exist or the attempt limit is reached.
notes: This file is the compatibility bridge that keeps legacy pickers populated from the registry while exposing save-as-type entry points.
questions: What exact registry Type schema is guaranteed for runtime, defaults, tags, slots, and provenance?
Should listener errors in CV_TYPES_PROMPT.open be observable anywhere?
Is _typeId on WS_BLOCKS intended only for display, or do downstream consumers depend on it behaviorally?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: registry types are converted into legacy array shapes, existing legacy entries are preserved, block definitions are annotated, and UI rebuild notifications are emitted.
apparent_themes: registry as source for legacy mirrors
intention_signals: It appears intended to let newer registry-authored types appear automatically in older picker UIs while keeping legacy globals, builders, and save-as-type workflows operational.
novelty: The file combines registry mirroring, boot-time retry coordination, legacy metadata hydration, and global save/prompt APIs in one adapter layer.
contribution: This file contributes a neutral translation layer between registry Type records and legacy global picker data, including merge rules, legacy preservation, block type annotation, rebuild signaling, and save-as-type helper entry points.
summary_for_embedding: Browser adapter that mirrors CV_REGISTRY Type records into legacy widget, system, wizard, and step globals, annotates block definitions, emits rebuild events, and exposes type promotion prompt helpers.
intention_for_embedding: This was for allowing registry-authored types to flow into existing legacy UI pickers and save-as-type workflows without requiring those consumers to change interfaces.

symbols.does: Converts a registry Type into a legacy widget kind object.
symbols.description: Reads defaults, runtime key, ID tail, name, description, provenance, and icon from a Type. It supplies default widget dimensions of 320 by 220 when width or height is absent.

symbols.does: Converts a registry Type into a legacy widget system object.
symbols.description: Maps the Type to the legacy system shape with id, typeId, label, description, provenance, icon, and slots. It uses the runtime key when present and otherwise falls back to the final segment of the Type ID.

symbols.does: Converts a registry Type into a legacy wizard kind object.
symbols.description: Builds a wizard kind using defaults.wizardKind or the Type ID tail as the id. It infers shape from tags for hexagon, circle, or octagon, falls back to diamond, and carries defaults.steps when provided.

symbols.does: Converts a registry Type into a legacy wizard step kind object.
symbols.description: Builds the legacy step kind fields from defaults.kind, ID tail, name, description, typeId, and provenance. It does not include shape, slots, dimensions, or icon data.

symbols.does: Returns the final dot-delimited segment of an ID string.
symbols.description: Splits the provided id on periods and returns the last segment. It is used as a fallback legacy id when a more specific runtime or defaults key is absent.

symbols.does: Rebuilds legacy globals from registry query results and preserved legacy entries.
symbols.description: Queries the registry for widget kinds, widget systems, wizard kinds, and wizard step kinds, converts those Types to legacy shapes, merges in unseen original legacy entries, updates window globals, annotates WS_BLOCKS entries with _typeId, and dispatches cv-registry-rebuild. It also hydrates wizard steps from matching legacy wizard kinds when registry data lacks steps.

symbols.does: Coalesces registry rebuilds onto the next animation frame.
symbols.description: Prevents duplicate scheduled rebuilds while one requestAnimationFrame callback is pending. When the frame runs, it clears the scheduled flag and calls rebuild.
